title: lathyrus ochraceus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Lathyrus ochraceus subsp. occidentalis, a lesser-known species of flowering plant, is a subspecies of the vetchling, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, Lathyrus ochraceus subsp. occidentalis has been prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
